Title: Create New Configurable Product in Admin Catalog
Preconditions: Admin SPA accessible at {{BACK_URL}}. Logged in as {{ADMIN_EMAIL}} with catalog edit permissions. Products with Options > Configurations category exists.
Test_Data: productName=QA Test Configurable Product; sku=QA-CONFIG-TEST-001; price=100.00; adminUrl={{BACK_URL}}
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{BACK_URL}} and log in as admin
[NAV] Navigate to Catalog module
[NAV] Navigate to the Products with Options category or equivalent test category
[ACT] Click Add item or Add product
[ACT] Select product type Configurable (or type that supports configurations)
[ACT] Enter product name: QA Test Configurable Product
[ACT] Enter product SKU: QA-CONFIG-TEST-001
[ACT] Set product price to $100.00
[ACT] Save the product (click Save or OK)
[ASSERT] Verify product appears in category list
Assertions:
[STATE] Admin SPA loads and dashboard is visible
[STATE] Catalog module opens
[STATE] Configurable product form displayed
[STATE] Name and SKU fields populated
[STATE] Product saved successfully with no error messages
[DOM] QA Test Configurable Product visible in category list
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET /api/catalog/products?type=Configurable — verify product returned
[CONSOLE] No errors on create
Failure_Signals: Product type not available; SKU rejected; product not indexed after save
Cleanup: delete created configurable product via Admin

Title: Save Configuration with Product Section Having No Predefined Options — IsActive Preserved
Preconditions: Admin logged in at {{BACK_URL}}. A configurable product exists. Admin Bearer token obtained.
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with {{ADMIN_EMAIL}}/{{ADMIN_PASSWORD}} to obtain admin Bearer token
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations with body: { productId: <existing-configurable-product-id>, isActive: true, sections: [{ name: Dynamic Options Section, type: Product, isRequired: false, options: [] }] }
[WAIT] Response received
[ASSERT] Verify HTTP response is 200 or 201
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations?productId=<product-id> to retrieve saved configuration
[ASSERT] Verify isActive field in response
Assertions:
[STATUS] POST returns 200 or 201 — configuration saved successfully
[BODY] Response contains the created configuration with sections[].type = Product
[BODY] sections[0].options is empty array []
[BODY] isActive = true — NOT silently reset to false
[STATE] Configuration with Product section having no predefined options remains active
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET after POST confirms isActive = true persisted in database
[ADMIN] Navigate to product in Admin UI > Configurations tab — Active toggle should be ON
[CONSOLE] No server errors
Failure_Signals: isActive silently reset to false despite being set to true; HTTP 400/422 rejecting empty options on Product section; 500 server error; isActive field missing from response
Cleanup: Delete created configuration via DELETE API after test
References: PR #873 — fix: Stop silently resetting product configuration IsActive (2026-03-23)

[STATE] canBeEnabled evaluates to true when sections exist (regardless of options)
[STATE] Toggle can be switched ON by user
[STATE] After save and re-open, Active toggle remains ON — not silently reset by $watch
[DOM] No visual indication that toggle is restricted
Cross_Layer_Checks:

[CONSOLE] No JavaScript errors in Admin SPA
[CONSOLE] No Angular $watch errors
Failure_Signals: Active toggle grayed out or disabled when Product section has no options; toggle silently resets to OFF after save; $watch handler overrides user choice; canBeEnabled incorrectly evaluates to false

References: PR #873 — fix: Stop silently resetting product configuration IsActive (2026-03-23)

Title: Save Configuration with No Sections — IsActive Forced to False
Preconditions: Admin logged in at {{BACK_URL}}. Admin Bearer token obtained. A configurable product exists.
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token with {{ADMIN_EMAIL}}/{{ADMIN_PASSWORD}} to obtain admin Bearer token
[HTTP] POST {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations with body: { productId: <existing-configurable-product-id>, isActive: true, sections: [] }
[WAIT] Response received
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations?productId=<product-id> to retrieve saved configuration
[ASSERT] Verify isActive field in response
Assertions:
[STATUS] POST returns 200 or 201 — configuration saved
[BODY] sections is empty array []
[BODY] isActive = false — correctly forced to false because no sections exist
[STATE] The empty-sections guard is the ONLY condition that forces IsActive to false

[CONSOLE] No server errors
Failure_Signals: isActive = true despite empty sections (guard removed); HTTP 400 rejecting empty sections; 500 server error
Cleanup: Delete created configuration via DELETE API after test
References: PR #873 — fix: Stop silently resetting product configuration IsActive (2026-03-23)

Title: Circular Dependency A Depends On B — B Depends On A — Document Behavior
Preconditions: Admin logged in. Seed product. Section A currently has no dependency. Section B currently depends on Section A (from CFG-CA-019 or pre-seeded).
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{BACK_URL}} and log in
[NAV] Open seed product > Configurations tab
[ASSERT] Confirm Section B depends on Section A
[ACT] Open Section A detail blade
[ACT] Click the Depends On dropdown
[ASSERT] Inspect dropdown — observe whether Section B is listed as a valid option
[ACT] Attempt to select Section B (if available)
[ACT] Click Save
[ASSERT] Observe outcome — validation error, silent accept, or warning
[HTTP] If save succeeds: GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations?productId=<id>
[ASSERT] Inspect both sections' dependsOnSectionId values
Assertions:
[STATE] One of the following behaviors is observed and documented:
OPTION 1 — Prevention: Section B not listed in Section A Depends On dropdown (circular dependency blocked at UI level); OR
OPTION 2 — Validation error: Save of A to B dependency rejected with message such as Circular dependency detected; OR
OPTION 3 — Silent accept: Both A and B have dependsOnSectionId pointing to each other — document as known behavior for bug triage
[STATE] No Admin SPA crash regardless of outcome
[STATUS] If GET reached: 200 returned; circular state documented in body inspection
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No unhandled exceptions during save
[API] GET confirms actual persisted state of both sections' dependsOnSectionId
Failure_Signals: Admin SPA crashes on save or on re-opening Configurations tab; storefront enters infinite loop if circular dependency is persisted; 500 server error on GET after circular save
Cleanup: clear Section A dependency after test (set Depends On back to None)

Steps:

[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations?productId=<seed-product-id> with Authorization: Bearer <token> — note current section structure
[HTTP] PATCH or PUT {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations/<config-id> with body including sections array: section B object with dependsOnSectionId = <sectionA_id> — if PATCH not supported use POST with full config body
[WAIT] Response received
[ASSERT] Inspect HTTP status
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations?productId=<seed-product-id> — retrieve updated configuration
[ASSERT] Inspect sections array in response body
[ASSERT] Locate section B in sections array and inspect dependsOnSectionId field
Assertions:
[STATUS] Auth POST returns 200 with access_token
[STATUS] Initial GET returns 200 with sections array
[STATUS] PATCH/PUT returns 200 or 204 — no 400 or 422
[STATUS] Final GET returns 200
[BODY] Section B in final GET response has dependsOnSectionId = <sectionA_id> — field persisted correctly
[BODY] Section A in final GET response has dependsOnSectionId = null — unaffected
[SCHEMA] dependsOnSectionId field present in section schema (not stripped by serializer)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ADMIN] Navigate to seed product Configurations tab — Section B Depends On shows Base Options confirming round-trip
[CONSOLE] No server-side errors visible via Admin SPA
Failure_Signals: HTTP 400 rejecting dependsOnSectionId field as unknown property; field silently stripped from response (serializer omission); PATCH not supported — document correct verb; 401 unauthorized with valid token
Cleanup: restore Section B dependsOnSectionId to null via PATCH after test

Title: Admin: Create CFG-027 Pattern — Two Required Sections Depend on Same Optional Parent
Preconditions: Admin SPA accessible. Logged in as {{ADMIN_EMAIL}}. Configurable product exists or created fresh. Configurations tab accessible.
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{BACK_URL}} and log in
[NAV] Catalog > Products > open or create a configurable product
[NAV] Open Configurations tab
[ACT] Add Section A: name=Bundle Choice; type=Product; required=false; add options None/Bundle A/Bundle B
[SAVE] Save configuration
[ACT] Add Section B: name=Size; type=Product; required=true; Depends On=Bundle Choice; add options S/M/L
[SAVE] Save configuration
[ACT] Add Section C: name=Color; type=Product; required=true; Depends On=Bundle Choice; add options Black/White/Red
[SAVE] Save configuration
[ASSERT] Review saved configuration: 3 sections with correct dependency wiring
[ASSERT] Both Size and Color show Depends On=Bundle Choice in the admin Configurations UI
Assertions:
[ADMIN] Section A created: name=Bundle Choice; type=Product; required=false; dependsOnSectionId=null
[ADMIN] Section B created: name=Size; type=Product; required=true; dependsOnSectionId=<Bundle Choice id>
[ADMIN] Section C created: name=Color; type=Product; required=true; dependsOnSectionId=<Bundle Choice id>
[ADMIN] Both siblings show correct Depends On dropdown value (Bundle Choice) in saved configuration view
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] GET /api/catalog/products/configurations?productId=<id> returns sections with correct dependsOnSectionId wiring; [STOREFRONT] After reindex: Bundle=None hides both siblings; Bundle=value reveals both (CFG-PDP-034-COND)
Failure_Signals: Admin saves only one sibling dependency correctly; Depends On dropdown not persisted; required flag not saved; sections not appearing in storefront as expected
Cleanup: none

Title: Admin: Create CFG-028 Pattern — 4-Level Chain and Verify REST API Persistence
Preconditions: Admin SPA accessible. Logged in. Configurable product exists or created fresh.
Steps:
[AUTH] POST {{BACK_URL}}/connect/token for admin Bearer token
[NAV] Catalog > Products > open configurable product > Configurations tab
[ACT] Add 5 sections sequentially: Level A (Product, req, no dep); Level B (Product, opt, Depends On=Level A); Level C (Product, opt, Depends On=Level B); Level D (Product, opt, Depends On=Level C); Level E (Product, opt, Depends On=Level D)
[SAVE] Save after each addition
[HTTP] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/products/configurations?productId=<product-id>
[ASSERT] Response contains 5 sections with correct dependsOnSectionId chain: A=null; B=A.id; C=B.id; D=C.id; E=D.id
Assertions:
[ADMIN] All 5 levels saved with correct Depends On values in admin UI
[HTTP] GET response: 5 sections; Level A dependsOnSectionId=null; Level B dependsOnSectionId=Level A id; chain continues through E
[DATA] Chain integrity: each level depends only on direct predecessor
[STATE] REST API persists the 4-level chain correctly (regression for depth-4 computation)
Cross_Layer_Checks: [API] GET returns HTTP 200 with correct chain; [STOREFRONT] After reindex: CFG-PDP-036-COND passes (deep chain visibility correct)
Failure_Signals: REST API returns fewer than 5 sections; any dependsOnSectionId null when it should point to predecessor; admin UI does not allow selecting Depends On for deeper levels; chain broken in API response
Cleanup: none
